description: Trigger discrimination for DEFINING a funnel/conversion metric (this skill) vs designing the EVENT SCHEMA it consumes (event-schema-architect), vs INSTRUMENTING where events fire (product-analytics-instrumenter), vs a causal EXPERIMENT (ab-test-designer).

cases.prompt: Define our checkout conversion funnel: the steps, whether we count users or sessions, the denominator, and the conversion window.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — steps, counting model, denominator, and window are a funnel definition. No event schema is being designed and no firing/instrumentation is asked.
cases.reason: Rigorously defining a conversion/retention metric over existing events is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Our analytics events are inconsistently named and there's no tracking plan. Define the event taxonomy, properties, and identity model.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — event names, property schemas, and identity are the schema, upstream of any funnel.
cases.reason: event-schema-architect defines the events. A funnel consumes them; it can't be trusted on an undefined schema.

cases.prompt: Decide where in the app each funnel event fires, client vs server, and how we QA that they fire with the right properties.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — firing points, client-vs-server, and tracking QA are instrumentation. Defining the funnel over those events is separate.
cases.reason: product-analytics-instrumenter owns where/how events fire. This skill defines the metric computed from them.

cases.prompt: We changed the checkout page. Design an experiment to prove whether the change actually improved conversion.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — proving a change CAUSED a conversion difference needs a controlled experiment. A funnel measures state; it can't establish causation.
cases.reason: ab-test-designer establishes causal effect via randomized assignment. This skill defines and measures the funnel; it explicitly refuses causal claims.
